items.question_text: When the glideslope for an ILS or LDA approach is reported out of service, when must the aircraft be advised?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1a2: An aircraft conducting an ILS or LDA approach must be advised at the time an approach clearance is issued when the glideslope is reported out of service, unless the title of the published approach procedure allows for it.

choices.text: After the aircraft has intercepted the localizer.
At the time the approach clearance is issued.
When the aircraft reports the glideslope is inoperative.
On initial contact with the approach controller.

items.question_text: Where a STAR and an IAP connect at an IAF or IF, at what minimum distance prior to the fix must the approach clearance be issued?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1a4: Clear the aircraft for approach at least 3 NM prior to the IAF/IF and specify the name of the connection fix.

choices.text: At least 5 NM prior.
At least 3 NM prior.
At least 10 NM prior.
At the IAF/IF itself.

items.question_text: Radar facilities may clear an aircraft to any fix 3 NM or more prior to the FAF along the final approach course at an intercept angle not greater than how many degrees?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1a6: At an intercept angle not greater than 30 degrees.

choices.text: Not greater than 20 degrees.
Not greater than 30 degrees.
Not greater than 45 degrees.
Not greater than 90 degrees.

items.question_text: Except for visual approaches, when may an aircraft be cleared direct to the FAF?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1c: Do not clear an aircraft direct to the FAF unless it is also an IAF, wherein the aircraft is expected to execute the depicted procedure turn or hold-in-lieu of procedure turn.

choices.text: Any time when on a radar vector.
Only when the FAF is also an IAF and the procedure turn/hold-in-lieu is expected to be executed.
When the intercept angle is 30 degrees or less.
Only for RNAV-equipped aircraft.

items.question_text: If the intercept angle is 90 degrees or less and a procedure turn is depicted, what must ATC instruct if a straight-in approach is desired?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1e: If intercept is 90 degrees or less and a procedure turn/hold-in-lieu is depicted, instruct the aircraft to conduct a straight-in approach if ATC does not want the procedure turn. Phraseology: 'CLEARED STRAIGHT-IN (type) APPROACH.'

choices.text: Issue 'CLEARED APPROACH' and the pilot will fly straight-in.
Instruct the aircraft to conduct a straight-in approach using 'CLEARED STRAIGHT-IN (type) APPROACH.'
Clear the aircraft direct to the FAF.
Tell the pilot to proceed direct to the MAP.

items.question_text: For aircraft operating on unpublished routes, when may an approach clearance be issued?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1b: Issue approach clearance only after the aircraft is: (1) established on a segment of a published route or IAP, or (2) assigned an altitude to maintain until established on a segment.

choices.text: At any time as long as the aircraft is in radar contact.
Only after established on a published route/IAP segment or assigned altitude until established on a segment.
Only after the pilot has acknowledged the unpublished route.
After the aircraft has passed the last fix on the unpublished route.

items.question_text: During times when pilots report GPS anomalies, what must controllers do for aircraft requesting RNAV approaches?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1l: Request the pilot's intentions and/or clear for an alternative approach if available. Announce to other aircraft requesting RNAV approach that GPS is reported unavailable and request intentions.

choices.text: Suspend all RNAV approaches for the day.
Request pilot's intentions, clear for alternative approach if available; announce GPS unavailable to others.
Continue issuing RNAV approaches since WAAS provides backup.
Switch all aircraft to ILS approaches only.

items.question_text: Must controllers approve a pilot request to apply cold temperature compensation during an approach clearance?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1a7: Controllers must not disapprove a pilot request to cold temperature compensate in conjunction with the issuance of an approach clearance.

choices.text: No, controllers should deny cold temperature compensation to maintain standard altitudes.
Controllers must not disapprove a pilot request to cold temperature compensate with an approach clearance.
Yes, but only at high-altitude airports.
Only if the temperature is below -10°C.

items.question_text: When may the controller omit specifying the published approach altitude in the approach clearance?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-5: When vertical separation will be provided from other aircraft by pilot adherence to the prescribed maximum, minimum, or mandatory altitudes, the controller may omit specifying the altitude.

choices.text: Always; approach altitudes are never specified in the clearance.
When pilot adherence to prescribed max/min/mandatory altitudes will provide vertical separation from other aircraft.
Only when the aircraft is on a visual approach.
When the weather is VFR.

items.question_text: Circling approach instructions may only be given for aircraft landing at what type of airports?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-6a: Circling approach instructions may only be given for aircraft landing at airports with operational control towers.

choices.text: Any airport with a published circling approach.
Airports with operational control towers.
Uncontrolled airports only.
Military airports only.

items.question_text: When the direction of the circling maneuver is required, how must it be stated?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-6b: State the direction using eight cardinal compass points and specify a left or right base/downwind leg as appropriate.

choices.text: Using clock positions relative to the runway.
Using eight cardinal compass points and specify left or right base/downwind leg.
Using the nearest 10-degree increment.
Using the published traffic pattern direction only.

items.question_text: A side-step maneuver clears an aircraft for an approach to one runway with landing on a __________ runway.
items.explanation: Per 4-8-7: You may clear an aircraft for an approach to one runway and inform the aircraft that landing will be made on a parallel runway. Example: 'Cleared ILS Runway 7L approach. Side-step to runway 7R.'

choices.text: parallel

items.question_text: When may an IFR aircraft landing at an airport without a tower or FSS be approved to change to the advisory frequency?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-8: Approve a change to the advisory frequency when you no longer require direct communications. Phraseology: 'CHANGE TO ADVISORY FREQUENCY APPROVED.'

choices.text: Only after the aircraft has landed.
When the controller no longer requires direct communications.
Only after the aircraft reports the field in sight.
When the aircraft is on a visual approach.

items.question_text: Does an approach clearance automatically authorize an IFR aircraft to execute the missed approach procedure?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-9a: Except for VFR aircraft practicing an instrument approach, an approach clearance automatically authorizes the aircraft to execute the missed approach procedure depicted for the IAP being flown.

choices.text: No, a separate missed approach clearance is always required.
Yes, except for VFR aircraft practicing an instrument approach.
Only if the controller explicitly states it in the clearance.
Only when weather is below minimums.

items.question_text: If the alternate missed approach procedure is published and in effect via NOTAM, must the controller issue the procedure description?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-9c: If the alternate missed approach procedure is published and in effect via NOTAM, the procedure description need not be issued to the pilot.

choices.text: Yes, the full procedure must be read to the pilot.
No, the description need not be issued if published and in effect via NOTAM.
Only the first fix needs to be read.
Only if the pilot specifically requests it.

items.question_text: When must detailed approach information (initial altitude, procedure turn, final approach course, missed approach) be specified in the clearance?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-10: Specify this information when the pilot says he/she is unfamiliar with the procedure.

choices.text: For all approach clearances.
When the pilot says he/she is unfamiliar with the procedure.
Only for non-precision approaches.
Only for circling approaches.

items.question_text: VFR aircraft conducting practice instrument approaches must be instructed to do what on initial contact?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-11d1: VFR aircraft must be instructed to maintain VFR on initial contact or as soon as possible thereafter.

choices.text: Contact approach control on a different frequency.
Maintain VFR.
Report the field in sight.
Switch to the CTAF frequency.

items.question_text: For VFR practice approaches where a facility directive does NOT require IFR separation, what must the controller tell the pilot?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-11d4(a): Instruct pilot to maintain VFR and advise that separation services are not provided. Phraseology: 'MAINTAIN VFR, PRACTICE APPROACH APPROVED, NO SEPARATION SERVICES PROVIDED.'

choices.text: 'CLEARED FOR THE OPTION.'
'MAINTAIN VFR, PRACTICE APPROACH APPROVED, NO SEPARATION SERVICES PROVIDED.'
'CLEARED APPROACH, REPORT MISSED APPROACH.'
'PRACTICE APPROACH APPROVED, MAINTAIN OWN SEPARATION.'

items.question_text: Are VFR aircraft automatically authorized to execute the missed approach procedure?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-11e2: VFR aircraft are NOT automatically authorized. This authorization must be specifically requested by the pilot and approved by the controller.

choices.text: Yes, just like IFR aircraft.
No, it must be specifically requested by the pilot and approved by the controller.
Yes, but only during daylight hours.
No, VFR aircraft can never execute missed approaches.

items.question_text: At what point is an aircraft cleared for touch-and-go or low approach considered a departing aircraft?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-12: Consider as arriving until the aircraft touches down or crosses the landing threshold; thereafter, consider as departing.

choices.text: When the aircraft reaches the missed approach point.
After the aircraft touches down or crosses the landing threshold.
When the aircraft reports on the go.
After the aircraft passes the departure end of the runway.

items.question_text: Before an aircraft begins its final descent for a touch-and-go or low approach, what must be issued?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-12: Before final descent, issue the appropriate departure instructions per 4-3-2. Climb-out must include a specific heading or route of flight and altitude, except when maintaining VFR and contacting tower.

choices.text: The missed approach procedure for the approach being flown.
Departure instructions including heading/route and altitude (except when VFR and contacting tower).
The next approach clearance in the sequence.
The frequency change to departure control.
